By the end, your API will be able to charge buyers and AI agents for access.</p></div></header><div class="mdx-content @container/columns-container relative mt-8 prose prose-gray dark:prose-invert [contain:inline-size] isolate empty:hidden mb-14" data-page-title="Quickstart for Sellers" data-page-href="/getting-started/quickstart-for-sellers" id="content"><!-- --><span data-as="p"><strong>Note:</strong> This quickstart begins with testnet configuration for safe testing. When you’re ready for production, see <a href="#running-on-mainnet" class="link">Running on Mainnet</a> for the simple changes needed to accept real payments on Base (EVM) and Solana networks.</span>
<h3 class="flex whitespace-pre-wrap group font-semibold" id="prerequisites"><div class="absolute" tabindex="-1"><a href="#prerequisites" class="-ml-10 flex items-center opacity-0 border-0 group-hover:opacity-100 focus:opacity-100 focus:outline-0 group/link" aria-label="Navigate to header">​<div class="size-6 rounded-md flex items-center justify-center shadow-xs text-gray-400 dark:text-white/50 dark:bg-background-dark dark:brightness-[1.35] dark:ring-1 dark:hover:brightness-150 bg-white ring-1 ring-gray-400/30 dark:ring-gray-700/25 hover:ring-gray-400/60 dark:hover:ring-white/20 group-focus/link:border-2 group-focus/link:border-primary dark:group-focus/link:border-primary-light"><svg xmlns="http://www.w3.org/2000/svg" width="18" height="18" viewBox="0 0 18 18" fill="none" stroke="currentColor" stroke-width="2" stroke-linecap="round" stroke-linejoin="round" aria-hidden="true" class="size-3 shrink-0"><path d="M8.50001 6.827C8.14801 6.995 7.81801 7.225 7.52701 7.517L7.51701 7.527C6.13601 8.908 6.13601 11.146 7.51701 12.527L9.69201 14.702C11.073 16.083 13.311 16.083 14.692 14.702L14.702 14.692C16.083 13.311 16.083 11.073 14.702 9.692L13.771 8.761" stroke="currentColor" width="1.5" linecap="round" linejoin="round"></path><path d="M9.50002 11.173C9.85202 11.005 10.182 10.775 10.473 10.483L10.483 10.473C11.864 9.092 11.864 6.854 10.483 5.473L8.30802 3.298C6.92702 1.917 4.68902 1.917 3.30802 3.298L3.29802 3.308C1.91702 4.689 1.91702 6.927 3.29802 8.308L4.22902 9.239" stroke="currentColor" width="1.5" linecap="round" linejoin="round"></path></svg></div></a></div><span class="cursor-pointer">Prerequisites</span></h3>
<span data-as="p">Before you begin, ensure you have:</span>
<ul>
<li>A crypto wallet to receive funds (any EVM or SVM compatible wallet)</li>
<li><a href="https://nodejs.org/en" target="_blank" rel="noreferrer" class="link">Node.js</a> and npm, <a href="https://go.dev/" target="_blank" rel="noreferrer" class="link">Go</a>, or Python and pip installed</li>
<li>An existing API or server</li>
</ul>
<span data-as="p"><strong>Note</strong><br>
There are pre-configured examples available in the x402 repo for both <a href="https://github.com/x402-foundation/x402/tree/main/examples/typescript/servers" target="_blank" rel="noreferrer" class="link">Node.js</a> and <a href="https://github.com/x402-foundation/x402/tree/main/examples/go/servers" target="_blank" rel="noreferrer" class="link">Go</a>. There is also an <a href="https://github.com/x402-foundation/x402/tree/main/examples/typescript/servers/advanced" target="_blank" rel="noreferrer" class="link">advanced example</a> that shows how to use the x402 SDKs to build a more complex payment flow.</span>

<span data-as="p">When a request is made to these routes without payment, your server will respond with the HTTP 402 Payment Required code and payment instructions.</span>
<h3 class="flex whitespace-pre-wrap group font-semibold" id="payment-schemes-exact-upto-and-batch-settlement"><div class="absolute" tabindex="-1"><a href="#payment-schemes-exact-upto-and-batch-settlement" class="-ml-10 flex items-center opacity-0 border-0 group-hover:opacity-100 focus:opacity-100 focus:outline-0 group/link" aria-label="Navigate to header">​<div class="size-6 rounded-md flex items-center justify-center shadow-xs text-gray-400 dark:text-white/50 dark:bg-background-dark dark:brightness-[1.35] dark:ring-1 dark:hover:brightness-150 bg-white ring-1 ring-gray-400/30 dark:ring-gray-700/25 hover:ring-gray-400/60 dark:hover:ring-white/20 group-focus/link:border-2 group-focus/link:border-primary dark:group-focus/link:border-primary-light"><svg xmlns="http://www.w3.org/2000/svg" width="18" height="18" viewBox="0 0 18 18" fill="none" stroke="currentColor" stroke-width="2" stroke-linecap="round" stroke-linejoin="round" aria-hidden="true" class="size-3 shrink-0"><path d="M8.50001 6.827C8.14801 6.995 7.81801 7.225 7.52701 7.517L7.51701 7.527C6.13601 8.908 6.13601 11.146 7.51701 12.527L9.69201 14.702C11.073 16.083 13.311 16.083 14.692 14.702L14.702 14.692C16.083 13.311 16.083 11.073 14.702 9.692L13.771 8.761" stroke="currentColor" width="1.5" linecap="round" linejoin="round"></path><path d="M9.50002 11.173C9.85202 11.005 10.182 10.775 10.473 10.483L10.483 10.473C11.864 9.092 11.864 6.854 10.483 5.473L8.30802 3.298C6.92702 1.917 4.68902 1.917 3.30802 3.298L3.29802 3.308C1.91702 4.689 1.91702 6.927 3.29802 8.308L4.22902 9.239" stroke="currentColor" width="1.5" linecap="round" linejoin="round"></path></svg></div></a></div><span class="cursor-pointer">Payment Schemes: Exact, Upto, and Batch Settlement</span></h3>
<span data-as="p">x402 supports several payment schemes that control how charges are calculated:</span>
<span data-as="p"><strong><code>exact</code></strong> (default) — The client pays the exact advertised price. This is the simplest scheme and works on all supported networks — see <a class="link" href="/core-concepts/network-and-token-support">Networks & Token Support</a>. TypeScript supports every network; Go and Python support a subset — see <a class="link" href="/sdk-features">SDK Features</a>. Best for fixed-price endpoints where the cost is known upfront.</span>
<span data-as="p"><strong><code>upto</code></strong> — The client authorizes a <strong>maximum</strong> amount, but the server settles <strong>only what was actually used</strong>. This enables usage-based billing where the final charge depends on work performed (LLM token count, compute time, bytes served, etc.). Currently available on EVM networks only (Permit2), in TypeScript, Go, and Python SDKs.</span>
<span data-as="p"><strong><code>batch-settlement</code></strong> — For high-frequency or repeated micropayment traffic, the buyer funds a channel once, signs off-chain vouchers per request, and the seller settles onchain in batches (not every request). Each call still advertises a per-request maximum (<code>price</code>); you can charge actual usage up to that cap using the same <strong><code>setSettlementOverrides</code></strong> pattern as <code>upto</code>. See <strong><a class="link" href="/schemes/batch-settlement">Batch settlement</a></strong> and the <a class="link" href="/schemes/overview">Payment Schemes overview</a>.</span>
<span data-as="p">The examples in step 2 above all use the <code>exact</code> scheme. To use <code>upto</code> instead, there are two key differences:</span>
<ol>
<li>Set <code>scheme: "upto"</code> in your route config, where <code>price</code> becomes the <strong>maximum</strong> the client authorizes</li>
<li>Call <code>setSettlementOverrides</code> in your handler to specify the <strong>actual</strong> amount to charge</li>
</ol>

<span data-as="p">The <code>setSettlementOverrides</code> amount supports three formats:</span>
<ul>
<li><strong>Raw atomic units</strong> — e.g., <code>"1000"</code> settles exactly 1,000 atomic units of the token (for USDC with 6 decimals, <code>"1000"</code> = $0.001)</li>
<li><strong>Percentage of authorized maximum</strong> — e.g., <code>"50%"</code> settles 50% of <code>PaymentRequirements.amount</code>. Supports up to two decimal places (e.g., <code>"33.33%"</code>). The result is floored to the nearest atomic unit.</li>
<li><strong>Dollar price</strong> — e.g., <code>"$0.05"</code> converts a USD-denominated price to atomic units. This format works when you configured your route with <code>$</code>-prefixed pricing (e.g., <code>price: "$0.10"</code>). Token decimals are determined from the registered scheme. The result is rounded to the nearest atomic unit.</li>
</ul>
<span data-as="p">The resolved amount must always be <= the authorized maximum. If the amount is <code>"0"</code>, no onchain transaction occurs and the client is not charged.</span>
<h3 class="flex whitespace-pre-wrap group font-semibold" id="3-test-your-integration"><div class="absolute" tabindex="-1"><a href="#3-test-your-integration" class="-ml-10 flex items-center opacity-0 border-0 group-hover:opacity-100 focus:opacity-100 focus:outline-0 group/link" aria-label="Navigate to header">​<div class="size-6 rounded-md flex items-center justify-center shadow-xs text-gray-400 dark:text-white/50 dark:bg-background-dark dark:brightness-[1.35] dark:ring-1 dark:hover:brightness-150 bg-white ring-1 ring-gray-400/30 dark:ring-gray-700/25 hover:ring-gray-400/60 dark:hover:ring-white/20 group-focus/link:border-2 group-focus/link:border-primary dark:group-focus/link:border-primary-light"><svg xmlns="http://www.w3.org/2000/svg" width="18" height="18" viewBox="0 0 18 18" fill="none" stroke="currentColor" stroke-width="2" stroke-linecap="round" stroke-linejoin="round" aria-hidden="true" class="size-3 shrink-0"><path d="M8.50001 6.827C8.14801 6.995 7.81801 7.225 7.52701 7.517L7.51701 7.527C6.13601 8.908 6.13601 11.146 7.51701 12.527L9.69201 14.702C11.073 16.083 13.311 16.083 14.692 14.702L14.702 14.692C16.083 13.311 16.083 11.073 14.702 9.692L13.771 8.761" stroke="currentColor" width="1.5" linecap="round" linejoin="round"></path><path d="M9.50002 11.173C9.85202 11.005 10.182 10.775 10.473 10.483L10.483 10.473C11.864 9.092 11.864 6.854 10.483 5.473L8.30802 3.298C6.92702 1.917 4.68902 1.917 3.30802 3.298L3.29802 3.308C1.91702 4.689 1.91702 6.927 3.29802 8.308L4.22902 9.239" stroke="currentColor" width="1.5" linecap="round" linejoin="round"></path></svg></div></a></div><span class="cursor-pointer">3. Test Your Integration</span></h3>
<span data-as="p">To verify:</span>
<ol>
<li>Make a request to your endpoint (e.g., <code>curl http://localhost:4021/weather</code>).</li>
<li>The server responds with a 402 Payment Required, including payment instructions in the <code>PAYMENT-REQUIRED</code> header.</li>
<li>Complete the payment using a compatible client, wallet, or automated agent. This typically involves signing a payment payload, which is handled by the client SDK detailed in the <a class="link" href="/getting-started/quickstart-for-buyers">Quickstart for Buyers</a>.</li>
<li>Retry the request, this time including the <code>PAYMENT-SIGNATURE</code> header containing the cryptographic proof of payment.</li>
<li>The server verifies the payment via the facilitator and, if valid, returns your actual API response (e.g., <code>{ "data": "Your paid API response." }</code>).</li>
</ol>

Enhance Discovery with Metadata (Recommended)</span></h3>

<ul>
<li>If you run into trouble, check out the examples in the <a href="https://github.com/x402-foundation/x402/tree/main/examples" target="_blank" rel="noreferrer" class="link">repo</a> for more context and full code.</li>

</ul>

Test with Real Payments</span></h3>
<span data-as="p">Before going live:</span>
<ol>
<li>Test with small amounts first</li>
<li>Verify payments are arriving in your wallet</li>
<li>Monitor the facilitator for any issues</li>
</ol>
<span data-as="p"><strong>Warning:</strong> Mainnet transactions involve real money. Always test thoroughly on testnet first and start with small amounts on mainnet.</span>
<hr>

<ul>
<li>Installing the x402 SDK and relevant middleware</li>
<li>Adding payment middleware to your API and configuring it</li>
<li>Choosing between <code>exact</code> (fixed-price), <code>upto</code> (usage-based per settlement), and <code>batch-settlement</code> (EVM micropayments with batched redemption) payment schemes</li>
<li>Testing your integration</li>
<li>Deploying to mainnet with CAIP-2 network identifiers</li>
</ul>
